frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Early visual system shaped by adaptation to natural scene statistics.

frameworks.name: natural scene statistics
frameworks.one_line: Directional image regularities predict perceptual anisotropies at the blind spot.

core_question: Do directional asymmetries in natural scene statistics around the blind spot predict the observed anisotropies in perceptual filling-in?
key_assumptions: Early visual function reflects innate specification plus experience-driven adaptation to natural input
Image statistics sampled at the blind-spot region are informative about cortical filling-in mechanisms
manipulations: Orientation of filling-in test stimuli (horizontal vs vertical)
Measurement of perceptual filling-in vs tolerance to disruption
key_findings: Perceptual filling-in shows a horizontal preference
Tolerance in filling-in shows a vertical preference
Natural-image statistics around the human blind-spot location exhibit stronger horizontal than vertical coherence
Filling-in pattern aligns with the directional asymmetry of these image statistics
interpretations: The perceptual anisotropy at the blind spot is consistent with efficient coding of natural scene statistics
